repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dist_synth_control/v1 Theorem 1 and Conjecture 1: finite transport-LP DTE diagnostic after marginal identification

published_prior_art.cite: Russell2021: computational sharp LP bounds for functionals of the joint potential-outcome distribution; OberReynolds2023: OT endpoint values for joint-potential-outcome functionals

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ot_dte_marginal angle0 Conjecture 2: finite-support monotone-path diagnostic G_eta_P(D) equals zero exactly when the pointwise benchmark equals the path set; eid_dist_synth_control/v1 requested the finite LP/OT width diagnostic

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoRidder2019: finite-vector Makarov regions can be improved; Russell2021 and OberReynolds2023: general LP/OT endpoint machinery
FirpoRidder2019: numerical finite-vector improvements to stacked Makarov bounds, but not this overlap-envelope witness scalar

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The handoff promises strict_tightening against Russell2021 and OberReynolds2023, but §8 states only a DTE-specific specialization/diagnostic, not a strict tightening, iff frontier, or non-representability result against their published theorems; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The §8 novelty block cites Russell2021 and OberReynolds2023 by bibkey only for the flagship scalar; it needs section/equation-level anchors tying the missing diagnostic to their exact LP/OT claims.

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters / Formal setup
soundness_flags.one_line: The notation R^eta(P) is used for the restriction class in the locked block and R^eta(P;D) for the stacked pointwise report in §6, making §8 references to restrictions versus reports ambiguous.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1 / Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: G_eta_P(D) depends on an arbitrary choice of one rational direction per vertex normal cone, but the choice rule is not locked; numeric gap claims and the 0.05 witness are not invariantly defined.

soundness_flags.label: Corollary: Rank-invariant point identification
soundness_flags.one_line: If the deterministic rank-invariant coupling is added to Pi^eta, the pointwise endpoints collapse with the singleton path and G=0; it is not a support gap between the marginal-only band and that singleton under the stated definitions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Canonicalize the diagnostic: either include V as part of the locked object G(P,D,V), or replace it with the full dual-unit-ball support gap so the claimed Hausdorff margin is invariant.

Rewrite Conjecture 1 as a strict comparator theorem against FirpoRidder2019 and Russell2021/OberReynolds2023, naming the exact published finite-vector set and proving what new computable object is added beyond standard support-function separation.

Repair the rank-invariant sanity check by specifying whether R is computed before or after the added copula restriction; if after, state G=0, and if before, define a separate marginal-only report R_marg.

Promote the concrete witness from an existence sketch to an auditable finite table with endpoint values, exposing directions, and the computed support gaps; this would also address the flagship concrete-witness floor.

recommended_next_step: Revise the kernel around a canonical G and a comparator-anchored flagship claim; otherwise this remains a field-tier LP/OT diagnostic rather than a flagship novelty.
